generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Building Control System temperature sensor providing real-time environmental data for synchronization with the Digital Twin. Integrated into BIM environment during device modeling phase."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Building Control System occupancy sensor detecting presence and movement patterns within building spaces. Spatial distribution defined in BIM model with automated extraction via MDE principles."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance EnergyConsumptionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Building Control System energy consumption sensor monitoring real-time power usage across building systems. Data sampling frequency determined by Building Control Engineers during BIM integration."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
]

instance HVACStatusSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Building Control System HVAC status sensor providing operational metrics for heating, ventilation, and air conditioning systems. Data used for real-time synchronization with Digital Twin's control logic layer."
    DTDFVocab:producedData HVACStatusDataTransmitted
]

instance StructuralLoadSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Building Control System structural load sensor monitoring physical stress on building components. Integrated with BIM's geometric model for structural health monitoring capabilities."
    DTDFVocab:producedData StructuralLoadDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance TemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
]

instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyConsumptionSensor
]

instance HVACStatusDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HVACStatusSensor
]

instance StructuralLoadDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom StructuralLoadSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BuildingInformationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Static geometric and semantic backbone of the Digital Twin providing architectural, structural, and system components with spatial relationships. Serves as foundational representation for all runtime data integration."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDitto, UnrealEngineIntegration
]

instance EnrichedBIMModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Design-phase BIM model enriched with behavioral representations of Building Control Systems components (sensors, actuators, controllers). Used for automated artifact generation via Model-Driven Engineering principles."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHono, BIMToDTTransformation
]

instance OperationalDigitalTwinModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dynamic real-time replica of the physical building integrating geometric BIM data with operational sensor/actuator data. Supports multi-fidelity modeling for comprehensive infrastructure representation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo UnrealEngineIntegration, RealTimeDataPipeline
]

instance TemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time environmental temperature data collected from BCS sensors. Used for thermal performance monitoring and HVAC system optimization."
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDitto, UnrealEngineIntegration
]

instance OccupancyData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time occupancy patterns data enabling space utilization analysis and energy management optimization."
    DTDFVocab:fromData OccupancyDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDitto, EnergyOptimizationService
]

instance EnergyConsumptionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time energy consumption metrics across building systems enabling predictive maintenance and efficiency analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDitto, PredictiveMaintenanceService
]

instance HVACStatusData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time operational status of HVAC systems including performance metrics and fault detection indicators."
    DTDFVocab:fromData HVACStatusDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDitto, FaultDetectionService
]

instance StructuralLoadData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time structural load measurements enabling proactive maintenance and structural health monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:fromData StructuralLoadDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDitto, StructuralHealthMonitoringService
]

// C11: Enablers
instance EclipseHono : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Lightweight M2M service enabling secure, scalable IoT data ingestion for real-time connectivity between physical sensors and Digital Twin data pipeline. Supports bidirectional communication for operational data synchronization."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeDataPipeline, DeviceManagementService
]

instance EclipseDitto : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Digital twin framework managing state and context of physical assets as software-defined twins. Supports dynamic model updates and event-driven interactions between physical and virtual representations."
]

instance UnrealEngineIntegration : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "High-fidelity simulation and visualization engine providing photorealistic rendering of physical assets for immersive analysis, training, and stakeholder engagement. Enables interactive exploration of Digital Twin representations."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

instance BIMToDTTransformation : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-Driven Engineering transformation pipeline converting enriched BIM models into deployable Digital Twin artifacts. Supports automated extraction of structured representations for runtime integration."
]

instance RealTimeDataPipeline : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Centralized data exchange hub enabling bidirectional synchronization between physical building systems and Digital Twin models. Ensures continuous alignment of operational data with virtual representations."
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous observation of physical asset performance leveraging integrated static models and runtime data to generate actionable operational insights."
    DTDFVocab:provides BuildingPerformanceInsight, OperationalAlertAction
]

instance ScenarioTestingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Virtual prototyping platform enabling what-if analysis of design modifications, refurbishments, and operational adjustments before physical implementation."
    DTDFVocab:provides DesignOptimizationInsight, VirtualPrototypingAction
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Data-driven forecasting of equipment degradation and failures across building systems enabling proactive maintenance scheduling."
    DTDFVocab:provides MaintenanceForecastInsight, MaintenanceScheduleAction
]

instance EnergyOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Data-driven energy consumption analysis enabling optimization of building systems for maximum efficiency and sustainability."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyEfficiencyInsight, EnergyControlAction
]

instance StructuralHealthMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous structural integrity assessment using real-time load data to detect potential structural issues before they become critical."
    DTDFVocab:provides StructuralIntegrityInsight, StructuralRepairAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Immersive, photorealistic rendering of building operations enabling intuitive monitoring and analysis through interactive 3D representations."
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualOperationalInsight, InteractiveNavigationAction
]

instance FaultDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated identification of system malfunctions and performance deviations using real-time operational data and historical patterns."
    DTDFVocab:provides FaultDetectionInsight, CorrectiveActionAction
]

instance DeviceManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Centralized management of Building Control System components including sensors, actuators, and controllers with remote configuration capabilities."
    DTDFVocab:provides DeviceStatusInsight, RemoteControlAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance BuildingPerformanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Comprehensive operational metrics including energy consumption, occupancy patterns, and system efficiency enabling data-driven decision making."
]

instance OperationalAlertAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated alerts triggered by system deviations or performance thresholds requiring immediate attention from facility managers."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DesignOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Virtual testing results showing potential improvements in building performance through design modifications or system adjustments."
]

instance VirtualPrototypingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Execution of virtual scenarios to evaluate design changes before physical implementation."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance MaintenanceForecastInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive analysis of equipment degradation timelines and maintenance requirements based on operational data patterns."
]

instance MaintenanceScheduleAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated generation of maintenance schedules based on predictive analytics and equipment health status."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EnergyEfficiencyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time and historical energy consumption analysis identifying optimization opportunities across building systems."
]

instance EnergyControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated adjustment of building systems to optimize energy consumption based on occupancy patterns and environmental conditions."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance StructuralIntegrityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuous assessment of structural component health with risk indicators for potential structural failures."
]

instance StructuralRepairAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Recommendations for structural repairs or reinforcements based on health monitoring data."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance VisualOperationalInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Immersive visualization of building operations including real-time system status and performance metrics in 3D environment."
]

instance InteractiveNavigationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "User-controlled exploration of building systems through interactive 3D navigation interface."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance FaultDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identification of system malfunctions and performance anomalies through automated pattern recognition."
]

instance CorrectiveActionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated or recommended corrective measures to address detected system faults and restore normal operation."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance DeviceStatusInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time status monitoring of all Building Control System components including sensors, actuators, and controllers."
]

    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The DT system enables **control flows from digital to physical** through interactions primarily managed by **Facility Managers**, who utilize the deployed DT for real-time monitoring and operational adjustments. Commands are executed via the DT Framework, which acts as a central hub for **bidirectional data and control exchange**. While the document does not specify exact command types (e.g., HVAC adjustments, lighting controls) or message formats, the system supports **runtime configuration management**, allowing dynamic adjustments without full redeployment. Feedback systems are implied through the **holistic building life cycle management** approach, where operational data from physical assets is continuously synchronized with the DT. Conflict resolution and command validation mechanisms are not explicitly described, but the framework’s **modular architecture** and **standardized extension points** suggest support for integrating such functionalities. Authentication and authorization are not detailed, but the emphasis on **interoperability** and **accessibility** implies safeguards for secure interactions."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system enforces **real-time data exchange capabilities** to ensure **continuous synchronization** between physical building assets and their digital counterparts, enabling live operational monitoring and control. While the framework supports **runtime configuration management** for dynamic adjustments without full redeployment, no explicit synchronization frequencies (e.g., millisecond, second-level granularity) or latency requirements are specified in the provided documents. The temporal granularity is implied to be **sub-second** due to the emphasis on real-time interactions, though exact thresholds (e.g., <100ms) are not defined. The system prioritizes **low-latency bidirectional data flow** to maintain alignment between physical and digital states, with adaptability enabled via modular service integration and configuration updates at runtime."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system employs a **coordinated, role-based architecture pattern** centered on a **central DT Framework** that orchestrates data flow between enriched BIM models and operational twins. Key components include: (1) **Building Control Engineers** (responsible for device modeling and deployment initiation via BIM tools), (2) **Facility Managers** (interacting with deployed twins for real-time monitoring/control), and (3) **DT Engineers** (extending capabilities via custom service integration). Resource management is enabled through **runtime configuration management**, allowing dynamic adjustments without full redeployment. System-wide coordination relies on **bidirectional data exchange** and **modular extension points**, ensuring a **holistic life-cycle management** approach. The constellation emphasizes **interoperability** (via standardized interfaces) and **scalability** (from individual components to entire infrastructure systems)."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in the proposed model-driven deployment framework spans from **initial device modeling** to **custom DT service development**, encompassing the following phases:
  1. **Device Modeling and Configuration**: Building Control Engineers define **sensors, actuators, and controllers** within the **BIM (Building Information Modeling) environment**, addressing BIM’s static limitations for Building Control Systems (BCS) components. This phase establishes a **unified semantic foundation** for subsequent transformations.
  2. **Model Enrichment and Transformation**: Leveraging **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) principles**, the framework extracts **structured representations** from enriched BIM models, enabling **automated generation of deployable DT artifacts** with minimal manual intervention.
  3. **DT Deployment and Synchronization**: The framework supports **real-time data exchange** between the physical building and its digital twin, ensuring **continuous synchronization** of system states.
  4. **Operation and Monitoring**: Facility Managers interact with the DT to **monitor system states** and **operate physical infrastructure**, utilizing **interactive visualization** for immersive monitoring and control.
  5. **Custom Service Integration and Evolution**: DT Engineers extend functionality through **custom services and plugins**, integrated via a **modular architecture** with **standardized extension points**, allowing the DT to evolve with operational requirements.
  Transition management between phases is governed by the framework’s **modular and standardized architecture**, ensuring seamless progression from static BIM representations to dynamic DT operations. Governance is implicit in the **role-based access** (e.g., Building Control Engineers, Facility Managers, DT Engineers) and the **automated artifact generation** minimizing manual errors."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system addresses **fidelity challenges** arising from the static nature of BIM and the dynamic behavior of physical buildings through **semantic enrichment** and **runtime synchronization**. Key considerations include:
  - **Model fidelity**: Achieved by enriching BIM models with **BCS components** (sensors, actuators, controllers) and operational logic, ensuring the digital representation aligns with physical system capabilities.
  - **Validation and verification**: The framework emphasizes **validating the model** to mitigate **semantic drift**—the divergence between digital and physical states over time. Real-time data exchange and synchronization mechanisms are employed to align the twin with physical changes, though specific validation protocols (e.g., comparative analysis, correction workflows) are not detailed.
  - **Uncertainty sources**: Primarily stem from **static BIM limitations** and potential misalignment between modeled and actual physical behaviors, exacerbated by unvalidated sensor data or control logic.
  - **Quality assurance**: Relies on the **automated extraction of structured representations** from BIM and the modular architecture’s ability to integrate custom services for continuous monitoring and adjustment. Error detection is implicit through real-time synchronization, but explicit correction mechanisms (e.g., runtime adjustments) are not described."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT Framework serves as a **central hub** for **bidirectional data exchange**, integrating **Eclipse Hono and Ditto** as core components for **real-time communication**. The **modular architecture** with **standardized extension points** supports **heterogeneous system interoperability**, though specific **communication protocols** (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT, REST) are not named. The **network topology** is not explicitly detailed, but the framework’s role as a hub implies a **star or mesh-like architecture** for connecting physical assets (sensors, BCS) to the DT. **Bandwidth and latency requirements** are not quantified, but **real-time synchronization** suggests low-latency constraints. **Reliability and redundancy mechanisms** are partially addressed via **runtime configuration management**, enabling adjustments without full redeployment, though no failover or backup protocols are specified. **Security protocols** are not detailed beyond the implied robustness of the framework’s design."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT system is deployed using a **modular architecture** with **standardized extension points**, supporting **custom service integration** and **analytics**. The framework’s **runtime configuration management** enables **low-overhead updates** and **adaptability** without full redeployment, ensuring **maintainability**. While the text emphasizes **scalability** (from individual components to entire infrastructure systems) and **accessibility** for varied user expertise, it does not specify **hosting platforms** (cloud, edge, or on-premise). The **deployment process** involves **Building Control Engineers** (for device modeling and BIM-enriched model deployment) and **Facility Managers** (for operational interaction), with the DT Framework acting as the **central hub**. **Computational and storage requirements** are not detailed, nor are **scalability mechanisms** (e.g., load balancing, auto-scaling) explicitly described. **Availability/reliability assurance** is implied by the framework’s design but lacks concrete specifications."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin framework prioritizes **interoperability across heterogeneous systems** through **standardized extension points** and **data exchange protocols** compatible with BIM tools (e.g., IFC, COBie) and operational systems (e.g., building automation platforms). Integration patterns include **bidirectional synchronization** between physical assets and digital replicas, with dependencies on external systems like **BIM tools for device modeling** and **enterprise systems for facility management**. The framework supports **low-overhead updates** to maintain accessibility and scalability, while **non-functional requirements (NF_X)** explicitly address cross-system interoperability. Horizontal integration extends to **enterprise-wide Digital Twins** or other DTs via modular service integration, ensuring alignment with AECO domain standards and dynamic operational needs."
]
